feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Thousands of men with prostate cancer will now be offered high-powered radiotherapy on the NHS
entry.summary: Thousands of men with prostate cancer will now be offered high-powered radiotherapy on the NHS

entry.title: As Ebola cases hit 1,000, almost 3 million children and adolescents face rising risks in eastern DR Congo
entry.summary: Ebola has left over 130 children orphaned in Ituri, driving urgent need for protection and care

entry.title: Quarantine comes to an end for the last of the hantavirus ship passengers in Nebraska
entry.summary: The last eight Americans who endured 42 days in a specialized hospital quarantine unit after exposure to an unusual hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship that killed three people have left the Nebraska facility. U.S. Department of Health and Human Services off…

entry.title: 3 Health Benefits of Metformin Beyond Blood Sugar Control
entry.summary: Metformin is commonly prescribed for type 2 diabetes, but it may also offer additional benefits, including effects on weight, PCOS, and heart health.

entry.title: 6 health issues that may shrink your testicles — including a condition more common on the left side
entry.summary: Dr. Alexander Pastuszak, a urologist and male reproductive medicine specialist, spoke to The Post about what could shrink the jewels.

entry.title: I think I might have Covid. (updated x 2)
entry.summary: No, not Morse. Me. I was fine last night. Woke up this morning to a godawful sore throat and aching like I’d been beaten up in Tinyhands McFelony’s shitty cage match. Took some Tylenol and powered…

entry.title: Guest Column | Ask a Vet: Can I catch a virus from my cat?
entry.summary: Cat-to-human transfer is rare. But the emergence of new strains has raised concern.

entry.title: Seattle scientists seek answers as colorectal cancer trends younger
entry.summary: The disease is increasing hitting younger adults in the U.S. and in Washington. Seattle researchers wonder: Could exposures in the environment be a risk factor?

entry.title: 18 kids’ undiagnosable diseases solved by AI: ‘Total game changer’
entry.summary: With the help of these tools, children with rare illnesses and symptoms may now find answers in a much shorter time frame than before.

entry.title: Two patients with severe autoimmune disease remain relapse-free for over 15 years after stem cell transplant
entry.summary: Neuromyelitis optica spectrum disorder (NMOSD) is a rare autoimmune condition in which the body's own defenses turn against the optic nerves and spinal cord. This confusion leads to inflammation that can rob people of their vision, disrupt something as basic …

entry.title: 5 Foods Cardiologists Want Men to Eat More Often for Better Heart Health
entry.summary: Cardiologists share 5 foods men should eat more often for better heart health.

entry.title: TikTok videos are feeding an ‘illicit subculture’ with risks of cancer and dementia, warns doc
entry.summary: Despite the well-documented dangers, TikTok videos have been normalizing illegal vaping among young people, according to new research.

entry.title: A Copper-Based Drug Clears Buildup of Alzheimer's Proteins in Mice
entry.summary: Clearing out harmful waste is one of the brain's most important jobs, and when those clearance systems weaken, damage and disease can quickly set in.

entry.title: This Is How Long You Should Strength Train Every Week to Live Longer
entry.summary: Your weekly weightlifting routine is probably doing a lot more for your longevity than you might expect.

entry.title: Health clinic in St. Louis loses federal funding. CareSTL was already collapsing.
entry.summary: CareSTL Health, which serves low-income and uninsured patients, has already shuttered four of its five locations.

entry.title: 24-Year-Old Identical Twin Dies 1 Month After Being Given '6 Months to Live' amid Terminal Cancer Diagnosis
entry.summary: Caitlin Leggett was diagnosed with acute myeloid leukemia for the second time in May 2026

entry.title: A common vitamin could help fight one of the deadliest brain cancers
entry.summary: A clinical trial is exploring whether high doses of vitamin B3 could give patients with glioblastoma a better chance against the aggressive brain cancer. Scientists found that niacin may help revive immune cells that tumors shut down, allowing them to attack …

entry.title: A New Treatment for Gray Hair? Researchers Are Working on an Innovative Approach
entry.summary: Gray hair is a natural but inevitable sign that we're aging. But there may soon be a way to reverse the process thanks to an odd finding in cancer patients.

entry.title: I’m a Dentist, Here’s One Thing That Can Prevent Gum Disease
entry.summary: Dr. Soumaya Zinet told Newsweek the “root cause“ of gum disease and a simple way to prevent the incurable condition.

entry.title: In Utah, here’s why some measles exposure sites never appear in public alerts
entry.summary: A person who doesn’t know they are infected with measles may enter a business in Utah and unknowingly expose people they pass by or sit near — yet that business may never appear in public health alerts.

entry.title: 8 things to know about the gut microbiome and keeping yours healthy
entry.summary: Wellness influencers often talk about fixing a broken gut microbiome. And marketers sell tests and supplements to fix your gut health. Here's what what the evidence really shows about gut health.

entry.title: The 4 Best Plant Proteins for Better Blood Pressure, According to Dietitians
entry.summary: If you’re hoping to move the needle on your blood pressure, don’t overlook the power of plant proteins like beans, lentils, tofu, and more.

entry.title: Parks Canada working to curb spread of deadly white-nose syndrome in Alberta bats
entry.summary: Parks Canada crews are at work in Alberta's bat caves, spreading a blend of bacteria to try to save the flying night mammals from a deadly, and accelerating, fungal infection.

entry.title: How Instagram Filters Your Brain: The Alarming Way Endless Scrolling Alters Your Real-World Body Image
entry.summary: The act of endlessly scrolling through Instagram as the algorithm has you hooked, may be harming your confidence. The main cause behind this is the distortion

entry.title: Confirmed Ebola cases in Congo top 1,000. More than a quarter have died, officials say.
entry.summary: Confirmed Ebola cases in the outbreak in eastern Congo have reached 1,003, including 254 deaths, officials said, and tracing those who've been in contact with patients remains a major challenge.

entry.title: Common Vaccine Linked to 24% Lower Dementia Risk in Major Study
entry.summary: As we get older, we face an increased risk of both dementia and shingles – but what if a single vaccine could provide protection against them both? There's already convincing evidence that an older shingles vaccine, developed under the brand name Zostavax, ca…

entry.title: 8 common food preservatives linked to higher risk of high blood pressure and heart disease
entry.summary: Researchers found eight specific preservatives associated with higher blood pressure, including sodium nitrite commonly found in processed meats.

entry.title: We Know About Breast Self-Exams. Should We Be Performing Vaginal Self-Exams, Too?
entry.summary: Doctors weigh in on this practice and if it's something people should be doing regularly.

entry.title: The Best Time to Eat Breakfast for Insulin Resistance, According to Dietitians
entry.summary: Dietitians explain the best time to eat breakfast after waking, and just why it may have a big impact on your blood sugar.

entry.title: Everyone talks about postpartum depression — beware these other mental health issues after childbirth
entry.summary: Maternal mental health conditions are the leading cause of preventable maternal mortality and morbidity in the US.

entry.title: A Simple Family Activity May Boost Brain Function, Study Finds
entry.summary: Getting enough exercise is one of the most important things we can do for our health.

entry.title: 'Natural Ozempic' gelatin drink goes viral — dietitian explains what it actually does
entry.summary: Drinking warm gelatin before meals is trending as a budget-friendly appetite hack, but a dietitian says comparing it to Ozempic is a massive exaggeration.

entry.title: Mutant mice resistant to pest control found in Philly, its suburbs, and NYC. Are rats next?
entry.summary: A Rutgers study of rodents found mice with a mutated gene making them resistant to poisons. They also suggest that rats have enough cognitive sophistication to evade traps entirely.

entry.title: Men's health slumps in midlife – but these simple lifestyle changes will add years to your life
entry.summary: The stereotype says men ignore their health because they’re careless, but doctors don’t agree

entry.title: A blood test that screens for multiple types of cancer? It may be available soon
entry.summary: The FDA could approve the first blood test that can detect multiple cancers, around the end of the year, setting the stage for it to become widely available.

entry.title: Confirmed Ebola cases in Congo outbreak top 1,000 with 254 deaths, authorities say
entry.summary: Authorities say confirmed cases of the Ebola outbreak in eastern Congo have reached 1,003, including 254 deaths

entry.title: Man taken into custody after multi-county police chase
entry.summary: A man was taken into custody after leading police on a multi-county chase Sunday afternoon.The police pusuit started in Porter County

entry.title: How To Keep Ticks Out Of Your Yard This Summer
entry.summary: Make your yard a tick-free zone this summer by mowing the lawn, trimming shrubs, and getting rid of lawn debris. Plus, learn how to protect your skin outdoors.

entry.title: Shockwaves from routine military duties associated with long-term anger and violence
entry.summary: Veterans who worked in high-blast occupations, such as artillery or weapons training, show higher rates of anger and aggression. Using artificial intelligence to scan medical records, researchers found this occupational exposure carries a distinct behavioral …

entry.title: 4 Supplements That Can Damage Your Liver
entry.summary: Some popular supplements, like green tea extract and turmeric, have been linked to liver injury. Here's what to know and how to choose safely.

entry.title: GLP-1 drug tirzepatide may boost calorie-burning brown fat
entry.summary: New research suggests GLP-1 drug tirzepatide may increase brown fat activity, helping the body burn more calories beyond reducing appetite.

entry.title: Is hearing loss an Alzheimer’s warning sign? Research shows new risk factors
entry.summary: The number of people living with Alzheimer's disease is expected to double by 2060. New research is shedding light on warning signs and risk factors that could signal cognitive decline ahead.

entry.title: Dementia Cases Climb, but We Aren't Powerless Against It
entry.summary: Bryan Walsh makes the argument in a piece for Vox

entry.title: Rare tick disease poses a danger in north-central Minnesota
entry.summary: Powassan virus attacks the central nervous system and can be fatal.

entry.title: These Lifestyle Changes Help Lower Your Risk of Chronic Disease For Decades
entry.summary: The Diabetes Prevention Program (DPP) and its long-running follow-up, the Diabetes Prevention Program Outcomes Study (DPPOS), have tracked thousands of people for more than two decades, examining how lifestyle changes can ultimately influence health.

entry.title: What to know about alpha-gal syndrome, the dangerous meat allergy caused by tick bites
entry.summary: Here is what to know about alpha-gal syndrome, the life-threatening allergy to meat triggered by tick bites.

entry.title: River Cleanup Employee Blames Cancer on the Work
entry.summary: Milwaukee Journal Sentinel explores the case related to the Fox River

entry.title: Research Suggests the Older You Get, the More Weed You Should Smoke
entry.summary: Research suggests that cannabis helps protect the aging brain against biological wear and tear and neurodegenerative diseases.

entry.title: For Millions, Ordinary Sounds Turn Into Excruciating Ordeals
entry.summary: Researchers push for official WHO code for misophonia as patients seek validation, treatment

entry.title: Is hearing loss an Alzheimer’s warning sign? Research shows new risk factors
entry.summary: The number of people living with Alzheimer's disease is expected to double by 2060. New research is shedding light on warning signs and risk factors that could signal cognitive decline ahead.
